milestone_brief:
Close UPL-1 (dark-mode stylesheet) from the 2026q2-graph-and-window frontend-uplift final report. Foundational candidate ranked #5 overall (RICE 9.75, MAJOR-graded by the challenger for the AI-12 re-audit work). 4-brief triangulation — surfaced by all four discover scouts as the headline visual gap (the dark VTK viewport meets the light Qt chrome at a hard edge that no peer scientific-viz desktop tool ships). Sequenced after UPL-2 (variety-palette-2026q2-e1, complete) so palette tokens are dual-defined from the start. Source: .claude/notes/frontend-uplifts/2026q2-graph-and-window/artifacts/final-report.md UPL-1 entry + Section 3 Foundational Candidates; challenger MAJOR finding at .claude/notes/frontend-uplifts/2026q2-graph-and-window/artifacts/challenge.md.

Track choice: Track A (build-ourselves) per the challenger's explicit recommendation. Do NOT adopt pyqtdarktheme-fork (Track B) — the challenger flagged the override-on-top maintenance risk where any future fork update could tighten specificity and silently override the app's custom QSS rules (resetDefaultsBtn pink, dock header style, etc.).

Scope:

1. styles.py — Add PALETTE_DARK: dict[str, str] parallel to PALETTE_LIGHT with key-identical entries tuned for a dark panel background. Existing TEXT_MUTED = #5a5a5a fails 1.94:1 on dark per the styles.py:62-63 comment; propose TEXT_MUTED_DARK (target #a0a0a0 or similar — clear WCAG AA 4.5:1 on the chosen BG_PANEL_DARK). All other palette tokens need dark variants: BG_PANEL_DARK, BG_DOCK_HEADER_DARK, BORDER_DOCK_HEADER_DARK, TEXT_VALUE_DARK, BORDER_GROUP_BOX_DARK, FOCUS_RING_DARK, BG_SURFACE_DEFAULT_DARK, BG_RESET_BTN_DARK, BORDER_RESET_BTN_DARK, BG_RESET_BTN_HOVER_DARK, TEXT_RESET_BTN_DARK, BG_VIEWPORT (stays #2f2f2f — it was already dark; this is the canonical baseline), COLOR_WIREFRAME_OVERLAY_DARK. Each value must (a) be 6-digit hex (AI-13), (b) pass WCAG against BG_PANEL_DARK at the appropriate threshold (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for non-text).

Derive APP_STYLESHEET_DARK by either: (i) parameterizing the existing f-string template to accept a palette dict argument so the same template renders against either palette, or (ii) duplicating the QSS template with token substitutions. Approach (i) is cleaner (single template, two outputs) and avoids the future drift risk where light-only changes accidentally don't reach dark. The Phase 1 researcher should propose the exact refactor pattern.

2. styles.py — Add VARIETY_DEFAULT_COLOR_DARK: dict[str, str] with the SAME 4 keys as VARIETY_DEFAULT_COLOR (Unicode en-dash + rho still required). Hex values should be tuned for the dark panel chrome where the swatch chip will sit — this also closes the deferred MF1 finding from variety-palette-2026q2-e1 (swatch chip contrast against BG_PANEL was below WCAG 3:1 in light mode; in dark mode, the swatch sits on BG_PANEL_DARK where the existing colors should pass at >3:1). Researcher: re-audit each of the 4 colors against the proposed BG_PANEL_DARK and either reuse the light-mode values or lighten/adjust as needed.

3. app.py — Apply APP_STYLESHEET_DARK as the DEFAULT theme on MainWindow.__init__. The viewport is already dark (#2f2f2f) so dark chrome is the coherent baseline, not the toggle. Add a Theme menu in the menu bar (or under a View menu) with Light / Dark / Follow-system options. Wire a _on_theme_changed(name) handler that calls QApplication.setStyleSheet(APP_STYLESHEET_DARK | APP_STYLESHEET) plus appearance_panel.set_default_color() re-seed from the active VARIETY_DEFAULT_COLOR_(DARK) dict. Follow-system uses darkdetect (already a runtime concern but no pip install needed — researcher to check if PySide6 6.6+ exposes a system theme signal natively; if not, fall back to a startup-time detect with no auto-refresh, documented as a limitation). NO QSettings persistence in V0 (that's UPL-25).

4. appearance_panel.py — set_default_color() needs to read from VARIETY_DEFAULT_COLOR_(DARK) based on the active theme. Add a theme-aware accessor: either a module-level get_variety_default_colors() that returns the active dict, or pass the theme as an argument to set_default_color. Researcher to pick the cleaner refactor pattern that keeps AppearancePanel decoupled from app.py's theme state.

5. tests/test_styles_palette.py — Extend with parallel-dark-bg WCAG assertions. Every existing text-contrast test against BG_PANEL should get a dark-bg twin against BG_PANEL_DARK. Every existing non-text-contrast test (UPL-12-style group-box border) should also get a dark twin. Add tests for VARIETY_DEFAULT_COLOR_DARK matching the existing VARIETY_DEFAULT_COLOR tests (key set, hex format, WCAG against BG_VIEWPORT — viewport is shared between themes since the canvas is always dark — and against BG_PANEL_DARK for the swatch chip 3:1 threshold).

6. CONTEXT.md — Add Section §4.X documenting the theme system: the dual-palette pattern, where PALETTE_LIGHT vs PALETTE_DARK applies, how APP_STYLESHEET_DARK derives from PALETTE_DARK, where the theme toggle lives, and the V0 'dark is default, no persistence' contract.

AI invariants:
- AI-13: 6-digit hex only.
- AI-12: WCAG AA 4.5:1 for text tokens against dark BG_PANEL_DARK; 3:1 for non-text. Re-audit EVERY text token.
- AI-11: qualified Qt enums for any new menu wiring (Qt.AlignmentFlag.*, QAction signals, etc.).
- AI-9: theme switching must not call processEvents() outside the existing _computing guard. The runtime theme swap (QApplication.setStyleSheet) is sync.

V0 scope:
- Dark as the launch default; Light/Dark/Follow-system available via menu toggle
- No QSettings persistence (UPL-25 territory)
- VARIETY_DEFAULT_COLOR_DARK closes the deferred MF1 from variety-palette-2026q2-e1 if the chosen colors clear 3:1 vs BG_PANEL_DARK; explicitly verify this and document
- No QtAds, no pyqtdarktheme-fork; pure Track A build-ourselves

Forward-compat:
- UPL-10 disabled QSlider QSS, UPL-12 group-box border WCAG, UPL-13 focus ring darken, UPL-22 viewport text overlay — all carry 'needs dark variant' TODO debt today. Their dark variants should ship in their own follow-up milestones, but the PALETTE_DARK structure must NOT block them — every token they reference needs a dark companion in the structure.

Validation:
- .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -q passes (must include new dark-bg WCAG tests)
- Manually verify by running app.py and toggling Theme menu — chrome should swap cleanly between light/dark with no flicker; surface colors stay legible on either chrome; status bar messages remain readable
- Off-screen render verification per CONTEXT.md §10: render the canonical surfaces with the dark default and confirm they look right against the new dark chrome
research_synthesis: Sonnet researcher proposed a 19-token PALETTE_DARK with BG_PANEL_DARK=#252526 anchor (VS Code sidebar register), every text token WCAG-verified (TEXT_VALUE_DARK=#e0e0e0 at 11.6:1, TEXT_MUTED_DARK=#a0a0a0 at 5.86:1, etc). _render_stylesheet(palette) function extraction from the APP_STYLESHEET f-string gives single-template two-outputs. VARIETY_DEFAULT_COLOR_DARK reuses the same 4 light values (all clear 3:1 on BG_PANEL_DARK) — closes deferred MF1 from variety-palette-2026q2-e1. Theme menu via QAction+QActionGroup in app.py (new menubar); Follow-system uses QGuiApplication.styleHints().colorScheme() + colorSchemeChanged signal (no darkdetect dep, Qt 6.5+ native). Pattern A accessor styles.get_variety_default_colors(theme) keeps AppearancePanel decoupled. ~270 LOC across 5 files — inline. Pre-existing AI-11 violation at app.py:475 (Qt.AA_ShareOpenGLContexts) explicitly out-of-scope.
